title: How much did Seele know about Rei pre ep23
display_title: How much did Seele know about Rei pre ep23
wikitext:
[]: How much did Seele know about Rei pre-ep23?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 21, 2009 6:46 am From the platinum subtitles: IKARI: That's right. The First Child will be maintained in her current condition. There is no need for new restrictions. The same goes for the Second and the Third. They only need to be watched. FUYUTSUKI: But Chairman Kiel and the rest will raise a fuss when they learn that Rei is alive. IKARI: I have offered something else to Seele's old men. We needn't worry.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 21, 2009 7:14 am I don't think they knew, no. They probably thought Lilith was still in her old body, that Rei was indeed someone Gendo picked up and decided to mold into the perfect soldier, and that 00 contained the soul of the now-orphaned girl's mother. They might even have suspected that Rei was Gendo's daughter.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Tue Jul 21, 2009 8:09 am I don't think seele really cared about the pilots. The probably didn't know Rei was mysterious at all. Nerv has a habit of randomly erasing record after all.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Jul 21, 2009 9:01 am I assumed Seele knew all about Rei. But I've also wondered about this question before. I mean, maybe Rei being alive had something to do with Gendo, and they could tell his agenda was different from theirs. Maybe they knew she wasn't needed as a pilot at that stage, so to put another Rei into existence would seem fishy to them. Aaaa I don't know.

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Tue Jul 21, 2009 9:56 am most likely, SEELE knew about Rei and what she was but became aware of her true purpose when Gendo brought in Rei III in that they realized Gendo had his own 3I agenda. i highly doubt they knew about Rei I's death as it was easily covered up.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 21, 2009 10:01 am If Seele knew about Rei they probably would have included her somewhere in Kaworu's list of instructions.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Tue Jul 21, 2009 10:43 am SEELE are Shinji/Asuka shippers

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jul 21, 2009 11:01 am 1. If Seele didn't know that Rei was the vessel of Lilith (which is likely, since she has no formal Angel designation), finding that Rei was alive would signal that something was terribly wrong, and that Lilith wasn't in her old body. 2. Even if Seele knew what Rei was, they probably didn't want Gendo to keep multiple bodies for her, because he'd be able to sneak her all-important soul off to another body and make a getaway if he had to. Having multiple copies gave him freedom to use Rei much more recklessly than he could have, and although Gendo wouldn't want to lose Rei, Seele doesn't see things his way and would see multiple copies as a sign of dangerous recklessness.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 21, 2009 12:29 pm I was under the impression that Seele knew of Rei as a pilot, but had no idea she was Lilith, and had no idea she had spare clones. After all, Seele WAS going to question Rei (3) in episode 23' until Ritsuko was sent in her place and.... (not that Gendo knew that horrible thing would happen to her. )

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Tue Jul 21, 2009 1:55 pm not that Gendo knew that horrible thing would happen to her.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jul 21, 2009 3:23 pm If SEELE knew the nature of Rei, I would think they'd have referred to her as "the second Angel" or some such. Of course, that would kinda clue the audience in... EDIT: I would think they would raise a fuss that Gendo was tinkering in the Rei-quarium without permission. He's been doing a lot of off-script things lately.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 21, 2009 6:16 pm Wow, good answers in here! If SEELE knew the nature of Rei, I would think they'd have referred to her as "the second Angel" or some such. Of course, that would kinda clue the audience in...

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Tue Jul 21, 2009 6:33 pm e: Okay, so it's episode 23. This is actually a no-brainer. It's been discussed, but to simplify: SEELE: "The Unit 00 pilot has sacrificed herself. Tragic." SEELE: "Oh, she's not only alive [not understanding that they're looking at the perfect clone of a dead girl], but completely recovered in defiance of all the regenerative abilities we know about the Evangelions and their pilots?" SEELE: "This is an odd curiosity indeed. We must investigate further." And then NERV was completely screwed.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 21, 2009 7:17 pm I Accidentally Dracula wrote: e: Okay, so it's episode 23. This is actually a no-brainer.

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Tue Jul 21, 2009 7:28 pm I think the very line of discussion in 23 is reason enough to believe SEELE doesn't know Rei is part of some unauthorized pet project in Gendou's team.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Jul 21, 2009 7:47 pm If they didn't know, then how did Gendo get enough funding for the clone tanks?

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Tue Jul 21, 2009 8:15 pm Padding NERV's requirements for repair funding? Writing it off as generic Dummy Plug R&D? There's multiple things he could have done.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jul 21, 2009 9:47 pm She blew herself up and got better. I would wonder about this as well since most people can not do then even in the Eva world.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 21, 2009 10:11 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: She blew herself up and got better. I would wonder about this as well since most people can not do then even in the Eva world.

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 22, 2009 8:21 pm If Seele was aware she was a clone, and thus may have other clones, then "Rei" being alive and in perfect condition isn't an issue at all. Seele would just automatically assume "oh, ok, NERV replaced the old one."
